title: rosa pumila var. hispida
intro: Rosa pumila var. hispida, also known as dwarf wild rose or dwarf thorny rose, is a small shrub native to parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Rosa pumila var. hispida include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. This plant is often prepared as a tea or used in infusions, and its leaves and stems can also be dried and made into capsules or tablets for herbal supplements.
